title: Dad stuck in support nightmare after teen lied about age on Discord
description: Data dump confirms dad's suspicions that Discord knew teen's age prior to hack.

title: Rocket Report: Chinese version of Falcon 9 fails; Artemis depends on rapid heavy lift
description: “As space becomes increasingly strategic, access is no longer a luxury."

title: AI Podcasters Really Want to Tell You How to Keep a Man Happy
description: Videos of fake relationship guru podcasters are reinforcing gender tropes and racking up millions of views, all the while driving sales to AI influencer schools.

title: Constellations
description: I. We had crash-landed on the planet. We were far from home. The spaceship could not be repaired, and the rescue beacon had failed. Besides me, only the astrogator, part of the captain, and the ship’s AI mind were left.  Outside, the atmosphere registered as hostile to most organisms. We huddle

title: Meta’s New AI Asked for My Raw Health Data—and Gave Me Terrible Advice
description: Meta’s Muse Spark model offers to analyze users’ health data, including lab results. Beyond the obvious privacy risks, it’s not a capable stand-in for a real doctor.

title: Orion helium leak no threat to Artemis II reentry, but will require redesign
description: After leaks on Artemis I and II, Orion's next flight to the Moon will need new valves.

title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
description: The ChatGPT-maker testified in favor of an Illinois bill that would limit when AI labs can be held liable—even in cases where their products cause “critical harm.”

title: RFK Jr. rewrites CDC panel's charter, opening door to anti-vaccine quacks
description: ACIP's charter now full of anti-vaccine terms and welcomes fringe groups to CDC.

title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
description: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

title: AI on the couch: Anthropic gives Claude 20 hours of psychiatry
description: Mythos is "the most psychologically settled model we have trained to date."

title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
description: ChatGPT had reportedly been used to plan the attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University last April. The family of one victim has said that they plan to sue OpenAI over the incident.

title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
description: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now it's No. 5 — and rising.

title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
description: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world. Are real cybersecurity concerns a cover for a bigger problem at the frontier lab?

title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
description: Black Forest Labs has long punched above its weight in the AI image generation space. Its next move? Powering physical AI.

title: The Pro-Iran Meme Machine Trolling Trump With AI Lego Cartoons
description: Since the beginning of the Iran war, the group Explosive Media has released over a dozen viral videos mocking Trump and the US.

title: The Download: AstroTurf wars and exponential AI growth
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from over.  In 2001, Americ

title: Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from over.
description: A rare warm spell in January melted enough snow to uncover Cornell University’s newest athletic field, built for field hockey. Months before, it was a meadow teeming with birds and bugs; now it’s more than an acre of synthetic turf roughly the color of the felt on a pool table, almost digital in its

title: Desalination technology, by the numbers
description: When I started digging into desalination technology for a new story, I couldn’t help but obsess over the numbers. I’d known on some level that desalination—pulling salt out of seawater to produce fresh water—was an increasingly important technology, especially in water-stressed regions including the

title: Mustafa Suleyman: AI development won’t hit a wall anytime soon—here’s why
description: We evolved for a linear world. If you walk for an hour, you cover a certain distance. Walk for two hours and you cover double that distance. This intuition served us well on the savannah. But it catastrophically fails when confronting AI and the core exponential trends at its heart. From the time I

title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
